summary: Comprehensive backend + frontend testing for Campus Chat (UPES anonymous chat). 20/20 backend tests passed including auth (signup domain rules, duplicate, login, bearer token), profile, search, friends flow, DM, chats, group messages, 403 for non-members, idempotent DMs, and WebSocket real-time broadcast (User A's lounge message received by User B over WS). Frontend E2E in mobile viewport (375x667) succeeded: Welcome → Signup (rejects @gmail.com with correct backend error, accepts @upes.ac.in) → Onboarding (anon username + avatar shuffle works) → Tabs (Chats/Discover/Friends/Profile all mount) → UPES Lounge chat (input visible, send button works, message renders in own bubble). No critical issues detected. Minor: one deprecation warning from React Native Web ('props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents') — coming from a 3rd-party library and not from app code, low priority.

react_native_issues.issue: Console warning: 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents'. Likely originates from a vendor lib (react-native-web internals or expo component) rather than app source; non-blocking but worth tracking. Reference: browser console during onboarding → tabs transition

action_items: No functional fixes required at this stage; backend + frontend critical flows are green.

(Optional) Investigate the source of the pointerEvents deprecation warning — if it surfaces from app code (search repo for `pointerEvents=` props on Views), migrate to `style={{ pointerEvents: ... }}`.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is a single ~575-line file. Acceptable now, but if features grow, split into routers (auth, friends, chats, ws).
JWT_SECRET has a hard-coded default fallback in code (`campus-chat-dev-secret-change-me`) — currently overridden by .env in this env, so safe. Consider failing fast if not set in prod.

CORS is `allow_origins=['*']` with `allow_credentials=True` — combination is non-compliant per CORS spec; browsers may reject credentialed requests. Not blocking now since auth uses Bearer header, but tighten origins for prod.
WebSocket endpoint accepts the JWT via query param (visible in server access logs). Acceptable for MVP; document this caveat.
Frontend uses `EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL` (correct for Expo). Backend URL is sourced via env in `src/lib/api.ts` — verified loading via web preview.
seed_data_creation: Test users created on the fly with TEST_ prefix in email local-part (e.g. TEST_usera_<rand>@upes.ac.in). DM chats and lounge messages created during testing remain in DB.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend exposes /api/* including /api/ws WebSocket. Use EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL from /app/frontend/.env (no default). Test users must use @upes.ac.in / @stu.upes.ac.in / @ddn.upes.ac.in domains. Chat input testID is `chat-input` (not chat-message-input). Lounge chat_id is `group-upes-lounge`. WebSocket: connect to wss://.../api/ws?token=<jwt>, receives `{type:'ready'}` first, then `{type:'message',...}` events for any chat the user is a member of. To re-run tests: `pytest /app/backend/tests/test_campus_chat_api.py -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml`.
rca_of_issue: N/A — no bugs found in this iteration.
